metadata.title: ContextCoherenceBench: a benchmark for evaluating context coherence in retrieval-augmented generation
metadata.description:
Companion benchmark for the paper "When Better Retrieval Hurts: Context Coherence Drives Faithfulness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation" (NeurIPS 2026 submission).

Contains:

  • Adversarial coherence test set (~129 validated cases across four categories: drift, disjoint, contradict, control).
  • Coherence-paradox test pairs across SQuAD, PubMedQA, HotpotQA, NaturalQuestions, and TriviaQA, evaluated against three open-weight generators (Mistral-7B, Llama-3-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B) and three retrieval configurations (baseline, HCPC-v1, HCPC-v2).
  • Context Coherence Score (CCS) computed per query, alongside per-passage similarity and faithfulness (NLI) judgments.
  • Frontier-scale rows from Llama-3.3-70B and GPT-OSS-120B via Groq, demonstrating the paradox persists at scale.
  • Leaderboard schema for community submissions (leaderboard.yaml) with rules and submission template.

The bundle includes a CITATION.bib entry, an MIT-licensed LICENSE file, a HuggingFace-loadable layout, and a SHA-256 manifest for every shipped file (see metadata.json).

Reproducibility code is at https://github.com/Saket-Maganti/rag-hallucination-detection; an interactive Gradio demo is at https://huggingface.co/spaces/saketmgnt/sakkk.
metadata.notes: If you use this benchmark, please cite the accompanying paper (see CITATION.bib in the bundle).
metadata.method: Adversarial cases were generated with Mistral-7B-Instruct and filtered by an automated NLI validator (cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base) plus a length and overlap heuristic; the final yield is reported in the bundle README. Coherence-paradox cases are the per-query union of three retrieval conditions (baseline, HCPC-v1, HCPC-v2) computed with the released code at the commit tagged v2.0.0.
